title: DTQEM v16.0 – Synchronized Two‑Times Model (Effective Lindblad Framework for Measurement‑Controlled Tunneling)
description:
This release presents a clean, efficient 2×2 Lindblad model for quantum tunneling under continuous measurement. The observer strength E_ext (0 → 1) controls two effects simultaneously:
- Effective Hamiltonian: H_eff = (Δ/2)(1-E_ext)σ_x → reduces tunneling rate
- Lindblad operator: L = sqrt(γ₀ E_ext) σ_z → increases pure dephasing (true Zeno mechanism)

Key numerical results (Δ=3.5 meV, γ₀=5×10¹⁰ s⁻¹, t=20 ps):
| E_ext | P_right | Interpretation |
| 0.0 | 0.0532 | Free Rabi (destructive phase) |
| 0.3 | 0.2872 | Partial decoherence |
| 0.5 | 0.7943 | Synchronisation peak (times balanced) |
| 0.7 | 0.2774 | Near‑Zeno regime |
| 1.0 | 0.0000 | Complete Zeno freeze |

The model unifies wave‑particle duality, the quantum Zeno effect, and the quantum eraser under the concept of temporal balance: observer time V = E_ext, particle time D = 1-E_ext, with V + D = 1.

All code is open source, numerically verified (error ~2×10⁻¹⁴ vs. analytical Rabi oscillation), and ready for experimental validation. Future work will separate the two control parameters (α_H, α_L) for richer physics.
